items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. The flaw, rated 9.8 CVSS, affects all versions prior to 1.11.12, with a fix released on May 25, 2026, and active exploitation observed in the wild.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by disguising code as an image. While Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported release lines remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities. These could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Auth Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: This vulnerability, part of APSB26-05, is an Incorrect Authorization flaw in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. A low-privileged attacker can bypass security measures to gain limited unauthorized view access of data without user interaction.
